seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity + degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: output: keep the minimum-length connected-network goal but restrict the output to a spanning subgraph in which every junction has fan-out (degree) at most 3, turning the easy MST into the NP-hard degree-bounded minimum spanning tree so greedy vs. local-search strategies diverge.
title: Vineyard Drip Lines: Fan-Out-Bounded Irrigation Grid
strategies: backbone baseline: lay the pre-surveyed strip-sweep path connecting consecutive junctions 1-2-...-n (degree <= 2, always feasible)
degree-capped Kruskal: add candidate pipes cheapest-first, skipping any that would push a junction past fan-out 3 or close a cycle, then patch to the backbone if it fails to span
edge-swap local search: start from a feasible tree and repeatedly replace the most expensive pipe on the cycle formed by a cheaper unused pipe whenever the swap keeps all fan-outs <= 3
randomized multi-restart: perturb the pipe insertion order across seeds and keep the cheapest feasible degree-bounded network found
